from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # simple verb mapping for common predicates
    verb_map = {
        "city": "is in",
        "country": "is in",
        "latinName": "has Latin name",
        "nickname": "is nicknamed",
        "rector": "has rector",
        "academicStaffSize": "has academic staff of",
        "established": "was established in",
        "state": "is in state",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "hasToItsNortheast": "has to its northeast",
        "dean": "has dean",
        "location": "is located in",
        "numberOfStudents": "has",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "director": "has director",
        "motto": "has motto",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "has",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "has",
        "officialSchoolColour": "has official colour",
        "outlookRanking": "has outlook ranking",
        "sportsOffered": "offers sport",
        "wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy": "was given technical campus status by",
        "founder": "was founded by",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "religion": "has religion",
        "headquarter": "has headquarters in",
        "largestCity": "has largest city",
        "river": "has river",
        "hasToItsWest": "has to its west",
        "anthem": "has anthem",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "legislature": "has legislature",
        "sportGoverningBody": "has sport governing body",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "neighboringMunicipality": "neighbors",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "patronSaint": "has patron saint",
        "hasToItsNorthwest": "has to its northwest",
        "campus": "has campus",
        "president": "has president",
        "staff": "has staff",
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "has doctoral students",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "has elevation",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
        "longName": "has long name",
    }

    # noun additions for numeric predicates
    noun_map = {
        "numberOfStudents": "students",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "postgraduate students",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "undergraduate students",
        "academicStaffSize": "academic staff members",
        "staff": "staff members",
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "doctoral students",
    }

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        # remove surrounding quotes from object if present
        obj = t.object.strip('"')
        if t.predicate in noun_map:
            clause = f"{t.subject} has {obj} {noun_map[t.predicate]}"
        else:
            verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, None)
            if verb:
                clause = f"{t.subject} {verb} {obj}"
            else:
                clause = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {obj}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # join clauses into one sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

    # capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
